summary: Form-associated multi-value select with a persistent trigger label, selected-count badge, local search, rich flat options, grouped sections, loading, and clear-all behavior.
useWhen: The user may choose several independent values from one finite set.
A compact trigger should summarize selection with the field label and a count.
The option set benefits from local search, subtext, grouping, or disabled choices.
avoidWhen: Exactly one value may be selected.
Selected values must remain individually removable as persistent tags outside the popup.
The choices form a hierarchy with cascading parent selection.

alternatives.when: Exactly one value may be selected.
alternatives.reason: Select owns scalar form value and single-select listbox behavior.

alternatives.when: The popup contains actions rather than values.
alternatives.reason: Menu uses command semantics and closes according to action-menu expectations.

accessibility: Provide aria-label or aria-labelledby when no external label association supplies the accessible name.
The popup is a listbox with aria-multiselectable and every option exposes its independent aria-selected state.
Disabled options expose aria-disabled and are skipped by keyboard navigation and typeahead.
Each row uses a presentation-only ds-checkbox indicator; multi-select option rows never add a prefix icon alongside it, and the option remains the sole selection semantic and interaction target.
The trigger keeps its field label visible; the count badge is supplemental and hidden from assistive technology.
Use errorMessage with error so the trigger is described by the rendered alert.
states: Values is a controlled JavaScript array; dsChange emits a new array after each user toggle.
Selecting an option keeps the popup open so additional values can be toggled.
The count badge sits without a halo in a reserved suffix box matching the control icon size: 20px md, 16px sm, and 12px xs.
The popup search row stays md at every trigger size, using body-medium text, a 20px icon, and option-aligned horizontal insets.
Clear-all emits an empty dsChange and dsClear while preserving useful popup or search focus.
Native form submission emits one entry per selected value under the component name.

Use options for a flat list or sections for grouped choices. Multi-select option contracts intentionally exclude icons because the leading checkbox owns that row zone; hierarchical parent selection is deferred.
responsiveBehavior: Use width fill in forms and filter rows; the persistent trigger label avoids variable-width selected labels.
The popup matches the trigger minimum width, may grow for content, and clamps to the viewport.
Long option collections scroll inside the popup; virtualization and remote search are not part of this version.

customElements: Assign values, options, and sections through JavaScript properties; array values cannot be represented safely as attributes.
react: Pass values, options, and sections as wrapper props and handle onDsChange, onDsClear, and onDsOpenChange.
angular: Import DsSelectMulti and SelectMultiValueAccessor when integrating with Angular Forms.
